name: JEDI-2P
description: Two-photon optimized GEVI evolved from ASAP2s with improved brightness, photostability, and sensitivity under two-photon illumination.

spectrum.name: JEDI-2P
spectrum.note: JEDI-2P's real 1P excitation/emission spectra digitized from the origin paper (Cell 2022, Fig. S4J): excitation peak ~501 nm with a ~420 nm vibronic shoulder, emission peak ~519 nm. Replaces a previously stored curve that was byte-identical to ASAP4e and mis-attributed to the ASAP4 paper — JEDI-2P is ~10-12 nm red-shifted vs ASAP2s/ASAP4 due to the T207H (GFP T203H) mutation, so the inherited curve was wrong. The paper's 2P excitation spectrum peaks at 940 nm (Fig. 2J).

kinetics.note: Depolarization is bi-exponential (Table S2): τ_fast = 0.54 ms (87.5%), τ_slow = 7.78 ms (12.5%); amplitude-weighted mean τ_on = 0.875×0.54 + 0.125×7.78 = 1.45 ms. Repolarization is mono-exponential (τ_slow = '-' in Table S2), τ_off = 1.21 ms. HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp, −70→30 mV depolarization / 30→−70 mV repolarization, n = 11 cells.

Depolarization is bi-exponential (Table S2): τ_fast = 1.21 ms (88.3%), τ_slow = 12.0 ms (11.7%); amplitude-weighted mean τ_on = 0.883×1.21 + 0.117×12.0 = 2.47 ms. Repolarization is mono-exponential (τ_slow = '-' in Table S2), τ_off = 2.54 ms. HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp, n = 10 cells.

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 33°C under widefield 1P illumination (HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp, n=11; FORCE1s paper Table S1.4). Activation (−70→+30 mV): τfast 0.49 ms (78%) + τslow 2.4 ms (22%) → τ̄ = 0.78×0.49 + 0.22×2.4 = 0.91 ms. Deactivation (+30→−70 mV): τ 1.5 ms, best fit by a monoexponential (no slow component reported). Previously stored the fast component 0.49 alone; corrected to the weighted mean per policy.

JEDI-2P reference values measured in the JEDI3 paper (Land et al. 2026) under widefield 1P illumination, HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp at 33°C. On (−70→+30 mV activation) is the amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit in Table 1: τfast 0.55 ms (85.1%) + τslow 7.57 ms (14.9%) = 1.60 ms. Off (+30→−70 mV deactivation) is the monoexponential τ of 1.20 ms.

Cross-GEVI measurement from the ASAP5 paper (Hao et al. 2024 Neuron, Table S2; primary subject ASAP5), HEK293A cells imaged at 1888 fps under simultaneous voltage clamp. Activation (−70→+30 mV depolarization) is the amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 23°C: τ_fast=3.0 ms (94%), τ_slow=29.2 ms (6%) → weighted on=4.57 ms. Deactivation (repolarization) was single-exponential (fast 100%, slow not relevant): τ_off=3.8 ms.

Cross-GEVI measurement from the ASAP5 paper (Hao et al. 2024 Neuron, Table S2; primary subject ASAP5), HEK293A cells imaged at 1888 fps under simultaneous voltage clamp. Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 37°C. Activation (−70→+30 mV depolarization): τ_fast=0.7 ms (88%), τ_slow=30.7 ms (12%) → weighted on=4.30 ms. Deactivation (repolarization): τ_fast=1.2 ms (51%), τ_slow=13.6 ms (49%) → weighted off=7.28 ms.

dynamicRangeData.note: Peak steady-state response amplitude to a 100-mV step from a resting potential of −70 mV, HEK293A cells under 2PM (940 nm), room temperature. Fig. 2C scatter mean ≈ −48% (read from figure; exact value in Data S2, not available). The previously-stored −43 corresponded to the Fig. 2B F-V curve value at +30 mV, not the Fig. 2C peak step amplitude.

Peak response amplitude to a 100-mV step from −70 mV under widefield 1PM, HEK293A cells, room temperature. Fig. S4C scatter mean ≈ −45% (read from figure; exact value in Data S2, not available). Per the legend, JEDI-2P and ASAP3 produce similar 1PM step responses (n.s.).

ΔF/F₀ = −0.46 ± 0.10 (highest magnitude of the three sensors) for a 100-mV step (−70 to +30 mV, 100 ms) in ND7/23 cells under 1P wide-field illumination (470 nm LED, 10 mW/mm², 50 Hz), whole-cell voltage clamp. Measured alongside Jarvis (0.29) and pAce (0.41).

Response to a 100-mV depolarizing step (-70 mV to +30 mV) at 23°C in HEK293A cells under 2P excitation at 940 nm (Fig. 1h; value 41.8% reported in main text and Supplementary Fig. 3/Table 2). Measured alongside JEDI3hyp (51.5%) and JEDI3sub (63.5%).

JEDI-2P reference: response amplitude to a 100-mV voltage step (−70 mV to +30 mV) at 33°C in HEK293A cells under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm (Extended Data Fig. 1b, value 41.73%). 33°C counterpart of the 23°C Fig. 1h value (-41.8%). Compare ASAP5 35.20%, JEDI3hyp 51.72%, JEDI3sub 60.26% in the same panel.

sensitivityData.note: Mean single-spike response amplitude (−18.2% ± 4.8% ΔF/F0, mean ± 95% CI) measured in vivo in awake mouse visual cortex L2/3 with resonant-scanning 2PM at 440 Hz (main text; Fig. 5C/5D, Fig. S6A–C). NOTE: previously mis-attributed to Fig. 2E — 18.2% is the in vivo 2P value, not the in vitro spike-waveform value. The Fig. 2E in vitro 2P single-spike value is ≈ −26% (see separate entry).

Peak response amplitude to a single 2-ms-FWHM spike waveform (mimicking L2/3 cortical AP) in HEK293A cells under 2PM, room temperature. Fig. 2E scatter mean ≈ −26% (read from figure; exact value in Data S2, not available).

Peak response amplitude to a single 2-ms-FWHM spike waveform in HEK293A cells under widefield 1PM, room temperature. Fig. S4E scatter mean ≈ −24% (read from figure; exact value in Data S2, not available).
Peak -%ΔF/Fo response to a 2-ms FWHM single-spike waveform at 23°C in HEK293A cells under 2P excitation at 940 nm (Fig. 1j). Measured alongside JEDI3hyp (36.4%) and JEDI3sub (42.8%).

Mean single-spike response amplitude of JEDI-2P-Kv in Layer 2/3 cortical neurons of awake, head-fixed mice under ULoVE 2P microscopy (-28.6 ± 4.1% ΔF/F, mean ± SD; bright-to-dim, magnitude reported). Modality inferred as 2P from the note (ULoVE is a two-photon technique); source preprint not directly accessible.

Mean single-spike (isolated AP) response amplitude of JEDI-2P-Kv in Layer 2/3 V1 neurons of awake, head-fixed mice under ULoVE 2P microscopy (27.6 ± 4.1% ΔF/F, mean ± SD; bright-to-dim, magnitude reported). Measured as the in vivo benchmark for FORCE1s-Kv (92.7%).

brightnessData.note: Relative brightness under 2PM (Fig. 2I, HEK293-Kir2.1 cells at −77 mV): JEDI-2P ≈ 1.52, ASAP3 ≈ 0.90 (both normalized to ASAP2s = 1.0), giving JEDI-2P/ASAP3 ≈ 1.69 ≈ 1.67. The stored ratio matches the 2PM Fig. 2I values, not the 1PM Fig. S4I values (where JEDI-2P/ASAP3 ≈ 1.55); sourceFigure corrected from Fig. S4I to Fig. 2I.

Relative brightness under 2PM (Fig. 2I, HEK293-Kir2.1 cells at −77 mV): JEDI-2P ≈ 1.52 vs ASAP2s = 1.0, i.e. ≈ 1.5×. The stored ratio matches the 2PM Fig. 2I values, not the 1PM Fig. S4I values (≈ 1.4×); sourceFigure corrected from Fig. S4I to Fig. 2I.
mBeRFP-normalized brightness at ~-70 mV in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells under 2P excitation at 940 nm, normalized to ASAP5 median (Ext. Fig. 1c, n ≥ 3,500 cells per indicator).

photostabilityData.note: Widefield 1PM photobleaching at 15 mW/mm² in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells at −77 mV (Fig. S4G). Normalized fluorescence read from the curve: ≈ 0.55 at 1 min, ≈ 0.47 at 2 min, ≈ 0.36 at 3 min. The previously-stored 55% @ 2 min was inconsistent with the figure (≈ 47% at 2 min; 55% corresponds to ≈ 1 min); value corrected to 47% @ 2 min.

F/F0 remaining after 1 min at 40 mW/mm² (1P widefield, 470 nm, CHO cells), read from the JEDI-2P 40 mW/mm² bleach curve in Fig. 1F; falls to ~0.46 by 235 s. Measured alongside Jarvis (~92% at 1 min, far more photostable) and pAce (~80%) at the same 40 mW/mm².

JEDI-2P reference, in vitro (HEK293A) under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm, 100 mW at the sample plane over a 40 × 635 µm FOV for 6 min (FORCE1s paper, Fig. S1.2B). JEDI-2P (purple) normalized-2P-fluorescence curve reaches ≈ 0.32 at the 360-s (6-min) endpoint — bleaching faster than FORCE1s (≈0.57) and ASAP4e (≈0.60) in the same panel. Sample-plane POWER (not an irradiance), so this 2P entry is display-only and does not normalize to the mW/mm² standard.

photobleach.note: Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the JEDI-2P curve in the representative 6-min 2P photobleaching trace (Fig. 1e, 37-mW excitation at the sample plane, HEK293A cells, n=3-4). Bi-exponential fit (fast tau1, slow tau2). This is a 2P, power-only measurement (not mW/mm2), so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to 1P GEVIs.

Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Re-digitized from the JEDI-2P 40 mW/mm² curve (lightest salmon; legend swatch 220,127,175) in Fig. 1F (1P widefield, 470 nm, CHO cells), F/F0 over ~235 s. The curve decays smoothly and monotonically from 1.0 to ~0.47 at 235 s with no plateau; the t75 crossing (~44 s) was read from the digitized points and confirmed by a bi-exponential fit (a=0.292, tau=39.4 s, tau2=552.4 s, r2=0.9995), overlay-verified against the figure. Corrects a prior digitization that had a non-physical flat plateau at ~0.47 from 120 s onward (giving t75=47 with a degenerate tau2=2000 fit). 1P/40 mW/mm² (intensityMWmm2=40); the panel scales t75 to the 100 mW/mm² reference. Measured alongside Jarvis (far more photostable) and pAce at the same 40 mW/mm².

Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the JEDI-2P curve in Fig. S1.2B (2P resonance-scanning, 940 nm, 100 mW at the sample plane over a 40x635 um FOV, 6 min, HEK293A; normalized 2P fluorescence). 2P power-only measurement (not mW/mm2) so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to 1P GEVIs. Measured alongside FORCE1s/ASAP4e/JEDI-2P in the same panel (they cross-overlay).

Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the JEDI-2P (gray) slow-phase bleach trace in Fig. 3F (in vivo ULoVE 2P, 1045 nm, 22.5 mW at the brain surface) of the VADER1 paper, where JEDI-2P is the photostable reference. The slow phase follows a power law F(t)=a*t^b; the paper's stated slope is b=-0.056 (a free fit to the digitized low-contrast gray trace gave -0.061, within tolerance). F(60s)~0.81 after the rapid phase, which never reaches 0.75, so the 75% crossing (~234 s) lies in the slow-phase regime. JEDI-2P is markedly more photostable than VADER1 (slope -0.135) in the same panel. 2P power-only measurement, so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized.

Origin-paper 1P photobleaching (Fig. S4G) in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells under continuous widefield 1PM at 15 mW/mm² over 5 min (300 frames, n=4 wells). Digitized by HSV color separation, verified against the figure. All three traces have a fast initial photobleaching component (caption); JEDI-2P has the SMALLEST initial drop (to ~0.81) but the steepest overall decline, ending lowest (~0.22) — consistent with the paper noting JEDI-2P is 'slightly more photolabile under 1PM' (its photostability advantage is under 2PM, not 1PM). Because the t75% crossing (~9 s) sits in the fast-bleach transient, the half-life t50 ≈ 65 s (in the sustained phase) is stored instead. Biexponential fit. Companion curves in the same panel: ASAP3, ASAP2s.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, awake head-fixed, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the VADER1 paper: optical action-potential waveform width (full width at half maximum) of JEDI-2P-Kv single spikes recorded in vivo under ULoVE 2P microscopy: 1.4 ± 0.3 ms (mean ± SD), statistically indistinguishable from VADER1-tKv (1.2 ± 0.2 ms). Sampling rate 7.2 kHz (7142.9 Hz, two ULoVE volumes/cell); awake head-fixed mice, body temperature ~37°C. Spike width = FWHM of the spike-triggered average waveform after 20 kHz linear interpolation.

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, whole-cell voltage clamp, in vitro (2-ms FWHM command AP waveform)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the JEDI-3 paper (St-Pierre lab, Nat Methods 2026, primary subjects JEDI3hyp/JEDI3sub): optical FWHM of JEDI-2P's fluorescence response to a single-spike waveform, printed value from Fig. 1k. 2-ms FWHM command AP waveform, 2P at 940 nm, 23°C, HEK293A (n = 10). Data at 80 kHz, downsampled to 20 kHz; FWHM after 20-kHz interpolation. Narrowest of the three (vs JEDI3hyp 6.3, JEDI3sub 6.6; *).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse visual cortex, awake behaving, in vivo, soma-targeted JEDI-2P-Kv (ULoVE 2P)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the JEDI-3 paper: optical spike-width FWHM of JEDI-2P-Kv from real cortical APs recorded in vivo with ULoVE 2P (820 nm, 1 kHz frame rate) in awake mice (n = 13 cells). Digitized from the unlabeled Fig. 2d bar chart (±~0.1 ms); spike widths were statistically indistinguishable across indicators (JEDI3sub ~1.3, JEDI3hyp ~1.2). Exact values in the paper's Supplementary Table 2 (not available locally). Consistent with the independent VADER1-paper measurement above (1.4 ms @ 7.2 kHz).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, awake head-fixed, in vivo (ULoVE 2P), soma-targeted JEDI-2P-Kv
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the FORCE1s paper (primary subject FORCE1s, parentId jedi2p): optical spike full width at half maximum of JEDI-2P-Kv single spikes recorded in vivo under ULoVE 2P microscopy: 1.4 ± 0.3 ms (mean ± SD; n = 17 cells), 1.9-fold narrower than FORCE1s-Kv (2.6 ± 0.5 ms, n = 9) measured in the same panel (Fig. 3E). Layer-2/3 V1 of awake head-fixed mice at 7.1 kHz ULoVE acquisition; body temperature ~37°C. FWHM = time at half-maximum amplitude of the isolated-spike spike-triggered average after 20 kHz linear interpolation (Methods). Independent of the VADER1-paper measurement above (also 1.4 ms @ 7.2 kHz) — different paper, same agreement.

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, whole-cell voltage clamp, in vitro (2-ms FWHM command AP waveform)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the FORCE1s paper (primary subject FORCE1s): in-vitro optical response width — FWHM of JEDI-2P's fluorescence response to an isolated 2-ms-FWHM, 100-mV command spike waveform under whole-cell voltage clamp + 2P imaging at 33°C, HEK293A (n = 10). Imaging by resonant-scanning at 721 Hz. Digitized from the Fig. 1K dot plot (y-axis 2-6 ms, 95% CI bars): JEDI-2P mean ≈ 3.6 ms (±~0.1 ms), narrowest of the three (vs FORCE1s ≈4.9, ASAP4e ≈4.2). This is the response to a synthetic 2-ms command input (broadened by command width + kinetics), distinct from the in-vivo real-spike FWHM of 1.4 ms (Fig. 3E). No exact number printed; digitized.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, anesthetized, in vivo (2P, paired juxtacellular)
apWidthData.note: Origin-paper in-vivo optical AP width: the spike-triggered average optical waveform (Fig. 5E) reconstructed at 0.23-ms resolution (4.4-kHz effective sampling) had a time-to-peak of 1.7 ms and FWHM = 2.1 ms (Fig. 5D), measured from JEDI-2P-imaged regular-spiking neurons paired with loose-patch juxtacellular recordings in anesthetized mouse V1 L2/3. (The paired electrophysiological spike FWHM was 1.1 ms — that is the electrical AP width, not the optical one, so it is not stored here.) Value stated explicitly in the text.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, V1 cortex layer II/III, awake behaving, in vivo (JEDI-2P-Kv, ULoVE 2P)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured/cited in the 2Photron paper (Villette et al. 2024 bioRxiv, primary subject 2Photron-ST552): optical spike FWHM of JEDI-2P-Kv = 1.84 ± 0.46 ms [1.25-3.55 ms], reported alongside 2Photron-ST552 (1.42 ± 0.13 ms) and ASAP3 in awake mouse V1 layer II/III under ULoVE 2P microscopy (>3.5 kHz sampling). Stated explicitly in the main text; the JEDI-2P-Kv cohort is drawn from Liu et al. 2022 (n=34). 2Photron-ST552 spikes were significantly narrower (Wilcoxon rank sum p = 2.91e-05, n=16 cells, 5 mice).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cerebellar granular layer, fast-spiking Glyt2+ Golgi interneurons, awake behaving, in vivo (JEDI-2P, ULoVE 2P)
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value measured in the 2Photron paper (Villette et al. 2024 bioRxiv, primary subject 2Photron-ST552): optical spike FWHM of JEDI-2P from fast-spiking cerebellar granular-layer glycinergic (Glyt2+) Golgi interneurons in vivo, ~0.72 ms, read from the FWHM (ms) sub-panel of Fig. S9d (green JEDI2P-Kv bar, n=10; axis 0.5-1.5 ms; alongside Voltron2-ST552 ~0.85 red and 2Photron-ST552 ~0.65 black). ULoVE 2P at 1045 nm, >3.5 kHz sampling, awake behaving mice. This is a SUB-MILLISECOND value that sits below the ~1-20 ms apWidth sanity range because it comes from fast-spiking cerebellar interneurons (much narrower APs) rather than the L2/3 cortical pyramidal neurons of all the other entries here — it is a genuine cell-type-specific measurement, not a broadened cortical spike. Kept for completeness; not representative of JEDI-2P's typical cortical optical AP width (~1.4-1.8 ms).
